title: Expected number of records of a random permutation = H_n (Algorithm M average case)
statement_informal: The expected number of left-to-right maxima (records) of a uniformly random permutation of [n] is the harmonic number H_n. Modelled via sequential-rank vectors (c_i in {1..i}, uniform); position i is a record iff c_i = i. Proved without enumerating the n! permutations: linearity over the last coordinate gives Tot(S m) = (S m)*Tot m + m!, i.e. E[R_n] = E[R_{n-1}] + 1/n.

cost_model.notes: records(c) counts coordinates with c_i = i. E records (ranks n) := (Σ records)/|ranks n|, |ranks n| = n!. The recurrence Tot(S m)=(S m)*Tot m + m! (linearity over the last coordinate) gives E[R_n] = H_n in QArith via field.

references: Knuth, TAOCP Vol. 1 §1.2.10 — Algorithm M (finding the maximum); the average number of times the running maximum is updated is H_n - 1 (records counting the first element gives H_n).
Rényi — sequential-rank representation of a uniform permutation; records correspond to records in the rank sequence.
Verified twin of conjecture artifact tools/conjecture/results/algorithm-m-maxima.json (enumerated E[records] = H_n).
